creator: nc@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to cyclaero from comment #3)
You're welcome and thanks for the kind words!

I actually use GNOME on FreeBSD myself also, so I have an incentive to have a usable desktop.

Previously, GNOME on FreeBSD was poorly maintained, while KDE was very well maintained, so I tried switching to KDE but didn't like it. No offense to KDE fans, they have a right to like KDE the way I like GNOME.
